payload_excerpt.quote_session_33_addition: Session 33 addition (2026-07-12): the appellate record (Minn. Ct. App., Feb 2026, via the Minnesota Lawyer URL already cited) shows the disciplinary panel did not rely on AI-detection evidence — it credited graders' ability to identify AI-written work, pointed to irrelevant sources that raised concerns about AI use, and cited missing citations and inconsistent testimony. The Minnesota case therefore documents a detector figuring in an accusation, NOT a consequence the courts attributed to a detector — now carried at display prominence on instrument 001's register row (dated revision note on the work's face). Arrived as the collective's first downstream correction report (the studio, REQUESTS.md 2026-07-12); converges with the expedition's own FIELD.md note ("grader judgment, explicitly not detector output alone"). Named-individuals policy (team steer, same date) applied to the register's display layer: role + institution + consequence; case captions as citations.
